environment.operatingSystem: Documentation review through connected GitHub and official Microsoft documentation

validatedActions.action: Reviewed current repository contract and active AI control documents

Reviewed current official Microsoft guidance for MSAL.NET WAM and browser fallback, Microsoft Graph permissions, drive-item delta, throttling, file downloads, drive-item hashes, .NET servicing, self-contained publishing, and Windows Server 2019 lifecycle
Added non-conflicting Microsoft platform controls and aligned agent instructions, environment inputs, implementation phases, acceptance criteria, and project overview
Compared branch to main and confirmed documentation-only changes

approvedDocumentationOutcomes: WAM-preferred interactive delegated MSAL authentication with system-browser fallback
No ROPC, device-code flow, client secret, certificate, application permission, Microsoft 365 write permission, or Graph beta API in version 1
Endpoint-to-permission matrix with no unapproved directory permission
Opaque delta links, duplicate-item handling, and supported HTTP 410 fresh enumeration and reconciliation
Exactly one retry owner, Retry-After handling, bounded backoff, and correlation IDs
Temporary download URL isolation from Graph credentials and persistence
Range resume validation for HTTP 206 and safe restart after HTTP 200
Microsoft Graph sha256Hash is ignored; supported source hashes remain separate from local SHA-256
Self-contained releases require republish for runtime security updates and exact patch evidence
Windows and .NET support status must remain valid for Production Ready
unexecutedChecks: real employee UPN and OneDrive URL resolution
limitations: This evidence validates documentation alignment only.
Exact MSAL.NET, Microsoft Graph SDK, .NET SDK, and bundled runtime versions remain implementation and release decisions.
Microsoft documentation must be revalidated before affected milestones and releases.
